slides.description: KUM Services GmbH converts proven civil aircraft into customized platforms—delivering performance, reliability, and value faster than traditional programs.
Civil-registered platforms can ferry to your location and convert on-site—minimizing approvals, delays, and total time-to-operation.
From ISR and surveillance to cargo delivery and countering hostile high performance autonomous aircraft—configure the airframe to match mission needs, not the other way around.

approach.description: The conversion of civil aircrafts to high performance autonomous aircraft (HPAA) is the easiest and fastest way. Civil aircrafts equipped for special mission are built for many different usage and customer requirements can be met at an affordable price.

whyUs.lead: There are a variety of manufacturers offering ready-made systems for purchase. These systems do not always meet customer requirements and are very costly with long delivery times. For this reason, it is important to offer high performance autonomous aircraft (HPAA) according to customer needs and available resources.

cards.title: Availability of Aircrafts
cards.description: Civil platforms are available on the market and can be selected based on mission and resource constraints.

cards.title: Low Cost
cards.description: A simple conversion path avoids lengthy development and construction timelines and keeps programme cost affordable.

cards.title: Design is Approved
cards.description: The base aircraft design is already approved and proven in civil operation.

cards.title: Reliability Proven
cards.description: Reliability is proven by thousands of flying hours, with worldwide maintenance options and spare parts supply.

callout.imageAlt: Beech King Air 350 on the runway
callout.description: The aircrafts stay on civil registration and can be ferried to the place of operation with pilots. There is no need for special flight permits from authorities. Conversion to high performance autonomous aircraft (HPAA) is completed on site in a very short period, and the aircraft is not identifiable as HPAA.

aircraft.lead: Each platform is selected for operational suitability and a proven track record. Suitable airframes are converted and integrated into high performance autonomous aircraft (HPAA) for your mission.

aircrafts.name: CESSNA CITATION 525B
aircrafts.description: The Cessna Citation 525B is a light business jet with twin turbofan power, offering high cruise speed, high-altitude operation, and long range. We convert suitable civil-registered aircraft of this type into high performance autonomous aircraft (HPAA) for rapid coverage and response; airframes can be ferried to the operating location with conversion typically completed on site in a very short period.
details: HPAA integration preserves jet speed, FL450-class ceiling, and transcontinental range within a standard civil-airframe footprint.
Well suited where wide-area transit, time-sensitive repositioning, or high-altitude sensor lines of sight are required for autonomous mission profiles.
Mission equipment and avionics interfaces are engineered to your HPAA payload, certification path, and installation constraints.

aircrafts.name: CESSNA 208
aircrafts.description: The Cessna 208 is a high-wing single turboprop utility aircraft with a strong record in cargo, special mission, and austere-field operations. Selected 208-class airframes are converted into high performance autonomous aircraft (HPAA), using the flexible cabin and cargo door for sensors, mission equipment, and logistics payloads while retaining worldwide parts and maintenance access.
details: HPAA configuration leverages strong short-field and unpaved-surface capability for logistics, surveillance, and multi-role autonomous mission kits.
Turboprop economics and global support networks keep operating cost predictable for sustained HPAA programmes.
Electrical, structural, and datalink interfaces are defined for your HPAA mission computers, sensors, and power budget.

aircrafts.name: C-130J
aircrafts.description: The Lockheed Martin C-130J Super Hercules is a four-engine turboprop tactical transport with very long range and a high-volume cargo hold. Where programme scope allows, integration converts suitable airframes toward high performance autonomous aircraft (HPAA) operation for the largest payloads and mission-system footprints.
details: HPAA-oriented integration uses exceptional range and payload for strategic lift, large sensor suites, or heavy mission equipment in a single airframe.
Rear loading and roll-on/roll-off flexibility support pallets, vehicles, and specialized mission racks for autonomous mission roles.
Planning covers structural interfaces, power and cooling, EMI considerations, and certification-aligned milestones for HPAA systems.

aircrafts.name: Beech King Air 350
aircrafts.description: The Beech King Air 350 is a pressurized twin turboprop with long endurance, stable cruise, and a proven multi-mission record. Civil King Air–class platforms are strong candidates for conversion to high performance autonomous aircraft (HPAA) for ISR, special missions, and related roles, with global support and a mature maintenance ecosystem.
details: HPAA integration remains flexible for sensors, communications, and operational mission kits.
We engineer conversion and integration to match your performance, endurance, and maintenance expectations.
A strong option when you need long endurance and versatility in an HPAA configuration.

aircrafts.name: Pilatus PC-12
aircrafts.description: The Pilatus PC-12 is a versatile single-engine turboprop utility aircraft from Switzerland, known for short-field performance, a pressurized cabin, and multi-role use. Selected PC-12–class civil airframes are converted into high performance autonomous aircraft (HPAA) for executive, cargo, medevac, surveillance, and special-mission roles—combining turboprop efficiency with rugged field capability.
details: Crew and cabin layouts vary; dimensions and weights support HPAA mission planning. Conversion preserves the PC-12’s operational strengths while integrating autonomous mission systems.
Excellent short-runway operation from paved and unpaved strips supports HPAA deployment to austere locations.
Single PT6A-67P power and cabin/cargo flexibility are integrated with your HPAA sensors, datalinks, and mission computers.

aircrafts.name: Boeing 747

specs.label: Powerplant
specs.value: 4 × high-bypass turbofan (e.g. CF6, RB211, GEnx on 747-8)

specs.label: Availability
specs.value: Worldwide; since 1970 — freighter & select passenger ops

aircrafts.description: The Boeing 747 is an iconic wide-body airliner with a distinctive upper-deck “hump” and very high payload or passenger capacity. Where programme and regulatory scope allow, selected 747-class airframes can be integrated toward high performance autonomous aircraft (HPAA) operation for special-mission cargo, oversized payloads, or unique long-range roles—building on the type’s size, range, and reliability.
details: Representative capacity and weights reflect the base aircraft class; HPAA integration defines sensors, autonomy, and mission volume for your programme.
Passenger and freighter variants (including 747-8) offer different HPAA integration paths; freighter configurations support large mission bays and nose-loading where applicable.
Four high-bypass turbofans provide the power and redundancy baseline for HPAA mission systems and endurance.

aircrafts.name: Airbus A380

specs.label: Powerplant
specs.value: 4 × high-bypass turbofan (Engine Alliance GP7200 or Rolls-Royce Trent 900)

aircrafts.description: The Airbus A380 is the world’s largest passenger airliner, with a full-length double-deck cabin and very high internal volume. For exceptional programmes, A380-class platforms can be considered for integration toward high performance autonomous aircraft (HPAA) objectives—using that volume and long-haul efficiency for specialized payloads, mission systems, or non-standard roles subject to feasibility and approvals.
details: Representative capacity and dimensions reflect the base aircraft; HPAA integration tailors double-deck volume, fly-by-wire interfaces, and mission architecture to your requirements.
Full double-deck layout and hub-to-hub economics inform HPAA planning for large-scale sensor, relay, or special-mission concepts where appropriate.
Four high-bypass turbofans and advanced flight controls provide the baseline for long-endurance HPAA cruise and smooth ride characteristics for sensitive payloads.

applications.lead: Civil aircrafts converted to high performance autonomous aircraft (HPAA) can be used for the following operations.

cards.title: Cargo & Delivery
cards.description: Transportation and dropping of materials, possibly with a parachute.

cards.title: Defensive Operations
cards.description: Defense or attack of targets by dropping defensive measures.

cards.title: Range Extension
cards.description: Used as range extension system for cruise missiles.

cards.title: Adaptive Platforms
cards.description: Cost-effective unmanned systems for monitoring, disruption, defense, and precision operations in high-risk environments—built to adapt to dynamic mission demands.

partners.lead: University of Stuttgart and Part One-Forty Five GmbH provide academic and certified maintenance support for the programme.

items.label: EASA Part-145 Certified Maintenance & Repair Organisation
items.title: Part One-Forty Five GmbH
items.role: Patrick Kopfer · Neuhausen ob Eck Airfield
items.text: Part One-Forty Five GmbH is an authorized EASA PART-145 maintenance and repair organisation. The organisation maintains turbine powered turboprops and jets.

items.label: Academic Partner
items.title: University of Stuttgart
items.role: Institute of Flight Mechanics and Flight Control · Prof. Dr. Walter Fichter
items.text: The Institute of Flight Mechanics and Control (iFR) works in guidance, control and navigation of aerospace vehicles and instruments. Cirrus SR22 / D-EXPT is used as autonomous flight demonstration platform.

items.label: Lead Integrator
items.title: KUM Services GmbH
items.text: Prime contractor responsible for end-to-end project management, system integration, and customer delivery. Based in Konstanz, Germany. Direct contact: info@kum-trading.consulting

contact.lead: Describe your operational needs and we will prepare a tailored aircraft recommendation with full technical specification and commercial terms.

form.heading: Send an Enquiry
form.sub: All enquiries are handled with strict confidentiality. We aim to respond in a very short time.
form.note: Your information is treated with complete confidentiality and will not be shared with third parties.
